review: I have used SCF to report illegal signs on the public right-of-way for years. However, there are times when Public Works fails to do their job and then replies on the complaint that it has been done. This is particularly annoying when they turn off “Public commenting” so that there is only one way to respond, ….. and that involves making a new request. This has happened recently at 3946 Northside Drive in Macon, GA.  Public Works has falsely stated (3 times) that the illegal signs and the illegal sign under construction had been removed. As of right now, the steel sign posts and the wooden sign posts are still in the same place, the public right-of-way. Someone in authority needs to step up and take responsibility.
title: Mixed Results

review:
Should be called SeeClickBouncePuzzleBouncePuzzleMaybeFix

Requires a street address even when I’ve geo-located the problem on my phone, so if the problem is along a park, as they often are, this app tries to put in an address on another nearby street. And the best I can correct this is to manually type in an address of a home adjacent to the park, on the right street but a block away. After having to look up that address in Google Maps. 

My municipality has found it necessary to implement about a dozen questions/checks for an issue, and even if I haven’t answered them all (because they are off the screen in the app), the app app still lets me hit Submit, then gives me a big error message in little tiny type. You could say that’s the fault of my city’s implementation, but it still makes this app very difficult and time-consuming to use. I end up standing by the side of the road for 15-20 minutes trying to get it all straight.
title: Very clumsy, frustrating, time-consuming

review: This app does exactly what you need it to do and is much easier than figuring out the address where you see an issue on the fly. Only improvement that's coming to mind would be to have the option to pre-populate the type of report via your profile. For instance, I almost never report anything beyond graffiti. I'd love for my reports to default to graffiti so I don't have to scroll through the list of options every time to make the same selection.
title: Nice, easy app

review:
Has stopped working with my local authorities and GPS locks are also taking longer or don’t work properly to get current location since last update.  The chance of all municipalities in two states no longer working with your system is pretty far fetched, so I’m going to chalk it up to an issue with the last update and location services.  You need to fix this!!!

App needs to be updated to use new iOS 12+ photo attachment API which no longer requires apps to have static and individualized permissions to our photo libraries.  The new API that’s been around since iOS 12 and is now in 13 as well, allows for the app to be given access to any photo the user chooses without needing permissions to the camera roll.  Your app doesn’t work properly when the new UI tries to serve it the photo the user chooses.  This needs to be fixed.

Worse app icon ever.  Am I fixing my heart or problems in the city/county I’m in? 🙄
title: Needs Update

review: This is nothing but a copied and renamed version of Commonwealth Connect. I would be OK with that if they at least fixed some of the glitches and shortcomings of Commonwealth Connect. First of all, there’s no intuitive way to sort through issues in your neighborhood. Second, there is really no help through the app to explain certain things. For example, what do the different colors of the pins actually represent? Also, why is it that when I receive an email notification I can’t just click on the link and automatically open the app to that issue? Instead, it opens a web browser which is much more difficult to navigate on a mobile device. I feel like this app is an excellent start to improving our community, but it really needs a better GUI.
title: Just renamed Commonwealth Connect

review:
This app is great, as it allows townspeople to report issues in their neighbourhood.  A couple of things need improvement:

It would be a great improvement using the search function to be able to filter by nearest incidents, having the most recent reports at the top.  Currently they sort and filter by location.  One has to fish through the lot to find the newest.  If I filter by newest, i get reports from adjacent communities, and have to go fishing through to find those for my town.  There is no information on the list what town the incident relates to.

I realize this may be difficult, and may require some work.  Possibly set a radius (miles?) from current location? Or add some information to the list to know what town is reporting an issue.

The communities often do not seem to close fixed incidents.  I have an incident that was fixed but the town never closed the issue, even though it's been done for months. This is a town issue, and not an issue with the app.  Perhaps the user reporting can have the option to close the incident using the app.  That way more items might be closed.
title: Improvements

review:
Past:  great tool for reporting and getting minor issues resolved.
Present:  Poor tool where duplicate, ONGOING complaints are being erased and intentionally overlooked by the City ppl accessing this app.  They are finding reasons NOT to acknowledge and address your written, legitimate complaints. Trying to hide something... i don’t know what, but something. 

Post and post for help to no avail.  Several caring Complaints CLOSED with no resolution. This app is now a joke that is simply Not Funny. City taxes paid, but this City rudely stops you from posting a complaint until they review and revise what you are actually complaining about.  Would do better sending a News cam for exposure.  Trying to make Memphis a better place, but the real issues are covered up.  Unprofessionally awful in many ways!  You started this system- Why STOP now!
title: Good tool under scrutiny by City if Memphis staff responders

review: To report and to get results is usually quite good! Where are you are sorely lacking and have been for years is when you report back to say that the problem has been fixed you don’t receive or ignore that so 1065 days later I still get the same question on the same issue over and over again that’s where this app really needs to be fixed! Somebody is dropping the ball at the end and it’s frustrating.  And I’d like to add that, it doesn’t matter how many times I report back thatThe issue has been fixed… Still get the question a few months later asking if it has it been fixed…. some of my issues have been close to 2000 days waiting for you guys to get the message. So fix that issue and you’ll get five stars for me! And thank you for the survey‼️
title: Feedback

review: This apps seems useless.  I reported an illegal dump; big bundle of trash dumped in the middle of the road.  Several days later the app indicated that the issue was closed, but the trash was still there.  I called the office and they said that someone came out and pushed the garbage to the side to make the road safe.  WHAT?  I asked why did they not remove the trash.  He said maybe they didn't have the proper equipment.  WHAT?  WHAT did they think they were coming out for related to an illegal dump.  He said he would contact a supervisor and see if they can get this cleanup expedited.  Here it is a week later and still the trash remains.  I don't know if this problem is specific to Oakland, but I do know that it is very frustrating and suggestive of a dysfunctional app and city.
title: Is anybody home?

review:
When I attempted to report an issue, I filled out all the required fields.  There was no exact address - it was just in the middle of a road, no House number.  I listed the road name as the address.  It would not submit and kept popping up a field that said there was no location listed.  There was indeed a location listed, so I don't understand why it was giving me the error.  I had to use the Full Site (on a phone, which was a nightmare), and when I entered the exact same info, it submitted easily.
I cannot believe this is human error as I did complete all required fields and drop-downs in app reporting an Issue.  Please check into it.
title: Could not submit - bug in app?

review: Within days of reporting a damaged storm water drain with rebar exposed, the city installed a brand new one making that spot in our neighborhood where many people walk daily safe again. See Click Fix is not only a time saver, but since it is so easy to report issues, I am happy to keep submitting instead of trying to make phone calls and send emails. Wonderful tool!
title: Hazard fixed quickly!

review: I be found the app to be quite useful in reporting issues such as graffiti, potholes, and other public safety issues. Seems to have some ongoing odd GPS location issues where it defaults to a specific address. Also I've noticed that there are a handful of posters responsible for a disproportionate number of reported issues including some very fickle issues such as a single candy wrapper next to a public walkway. I think the app could benefit from some moderating in such instances.
title: Good app no some issues,

review:
I spoke with a clerk at the county of San Bernardino regarding an issue with the gravel along the road washing away, and was told to either email the issue with pictures or use the app.  The email address that I wrote down, came back undeliverable, so I was going to submit it on the app. The app indicates the address is not part of the county and would not let me proceed to submit the request.

The app needs to be corrected.  The address has been a part of the county for a very, very long time. It is not part of the city.  The address is located on Kendall between Yucca Street and Little League Drive and is part of the county responsibility.
title: Location of an issue indicates it is not part of the county area

review: Really wish this app had a way to edit or delete a post after submitting. At least twice I’ve accidentally swiped a finger (yes, my fault) and ended up with incomplete postings.  After hitting “submit” it would be very helpful if there was a message something like “Once submitted your post cannot be edited or deleted, are you ready to submit at this time?”
title: Can’t edit/delete post after submitting

review: I like being able to help our local authorities address problem issues in the community. What I don’t like is  when these problems are disbursed to other authorities or departments and are immediately closed. To me, closing means completed. Leave the original call open so the public can follow the process from reporting to finished. This will also help those who are about to report the issue again know that the situation is already in process and they can follothe process too if they desire.
title: Closing Early

review: This application is generally good for reporting information to the city. However, I would suggest adding the ability to add videos. Also, the reporting party should be the one to clear the report because the city has been clearing reports as “resolved” when they receive the report, but prior to carrying out the work. We should be able to hold the city accountable by keeping track of the length of time it took from reporting the issue, to the city actually carrying out the work.
title: Missing some tools

review:
It seems like the only goal of this app is to push an issue onto someone else. I submitted that an ottoman has been dumped at a bus stop. It went from one department to another before I was told by King County Transportation that it’s been there a while, so weee just going to leave it. Now there’s more crap over there. 
I just submitted a request for a broken or leaking water main at the Crestview Park. Just got a notice that it’s been closed because I should contact the water department. 

Useless. Glad we’re spending money on an app that is not maintained and only exists for some government employee to have one more box to check everyday.
title: Worse interactions ever

review: Living on this alley has made me realize that much of the damage is also due to the amount of traffic using it as a through street. Many patrons leaving the pharmacy head north instead of exiting to 9th. The alley is busier than Lincoln Street to the front of my house. My wife and I are both retired and see this daily out our backyard. A no left turn sign from the pharmacy might be in order.
title: Alley between 8th/9th

review: I like the ease of reporting and the overall functions of the app. However there should be a way for the government entities to not be able to close a report without verification by a third party or the person who submitted the issue. A county near me posts an automated answer with vague information and many times the issue is not actually handled.  Or they say they have handled it even though it’s an ongoing issue.
title: Automated messages

review:
The new version is pretty, but did little to enhance the user experience. 

The messages and notifications feed is no longer. 
You can no longer view user profiles to see issues they submitted. 
You can no longer see top contributors in your community. 

Some more minor, but annoying features that disappeared:
Way to many taps and swipes to see an issue image. 
No way to zoom in on an issue image. 
No way to tap on user profiles/usernames in the comment thread. 
No way to see total number of issues I reported. 
And more 

As you can see, many features lost, but few gained other than a new look for the same tasks. While I provided this feedback in the beta stage, unfortunately it wasn't taken into account. I hope these features are on the road map and that this app see more updates than the last one. 

Keep up the good work.
title: New Version, Lost Features

review: I don’t know what Public Works repaired on Wednesday evening but YOU GUYS ROCK!  We have lived in Skyline for 8 years. The next day, I had the best shower of my life in 8 years!! On Saturday I could run my dishwasher and washing machine at the same time and it didn’t take forever for both of them to fill.  Whatever you repaired made a huge difference!!!  Thank you for all your hard work.  Pam Balentine
title: Water

review:
See click fix, has been very useful to report things such as pot holes, unlicensed auto sales for. Homes and sidewalks that need clear g.
I suggest we have a field that allow us to report vehicles registers out of state but always on mlocla property. This type of w braking causes oss of $1.3M in Stamford and that is not catching all
Of them.
Aunt some similar way we can report specifically for out of state vehicle, that don't pau local insurance, race, etc.
title: Useful resource most of the time- w/ suggestion License Plates tax evaders out of state.

review:
The city of Marlborough MA uses this app. I’ve reported a couple of issues and they always respond within the same day. Not necessarily to fix the issue but to let me know it’s on their agenda. Certainly useful in closing the time between rude things happening and getting a fix. 

A great public service tool!
title: It’s a local and mobile thing

review: I can not report anything that asked for an address as on my phone I can not edit the location. All I see when I go to edit it is the lengthy generic text the app has. I can not scroll to the bottoms to edit it. My only choice is to report using the wrong type of report saying everything is a road issue or a park issue even when it is with a property. I never had an issue in the past being able to enter the exact address. Hope this gets solved as I like the app and my city responds well to it.
title: Used to work well, now not so much

review: It is a great way to see what issues are going on on in our community’s and other community’s and stay up to date on the current issues it is also a smooth and non-complicated process to report issues and also runs smooth and doesn’t crash my phone
title: Great app to use and in joy

review: This app has made me have faith in the City of Vallejo. You record something on this app and presto it gets taken care of. Seems like it’s a great way to show accountability for the employees & you visual see things get taken care of or fixed. Makes you want to keep paying your city taxes & thank the employees for doing their jobs for us tax payers.
title: City of Vallejo

review:
This app, unfortunately, is down to having only a single developer working on it and he’s limited to just 30min per week. 

If you try to use punctuation when opening a ticket, the app will remove it when you hit post. As a result, you’re going to look like an angry and illiterate lunatic when you submit your request. 

This has been a bug for years. The latest iteration of the bug removes all punctuation from your ticket and then just appends it to the end as an unbroken series of periods, commas, and question marks.
title: Years later, punctuation is still broken

review:
The previous reporting app used by Multnomah County was cumbersome and very difficult to navigate. Click/Fix is fantastic. Some of the best features are: 1)submitting a photo also provides the exact location
2)  logical screen sequence to report an issue
3) immediate submission acknowledgement and subsequent followup 
4) option to share posting with impacted neighbors
title: Extremely user friendly

review:
You guys are great I just wish you had more funding and more people working for you so you can make the streets of Vallejo a lot cleaner and better!
I mean Dang Vallejo had two presidential candidates come to our town and we have to do better than this.
So thank you see it Click it fix it! I appreciate what you do...
title: My Rubbish review

review: It was better before they improved it. There’s very little information and it’s hard to find. It seems rather static and of little use. even when things get done it’s hard to track when they got done. It’s sort of an after the fact reporting. All you can tell is that it got assigned to somebody but that was obvious before. it used to be much more interactive.
title: More confusion, less information

review: The people in charge of this app could have also installed a delete button, to remove old requests over a year old. I keep getting emails on an outstanding request from 120+ days ago, just wanted it deleted. I reached out and they said they are not able to delete resolved requests. Why not?? Old resolved requests should be deleted. Please install a delete button and I’ll change my rating.
title: Good utility app but…

review:
1)  Put your marker in a specific point. When you are done, your marker is always offset to the left of where you placed it. Sometimes that can shift it to another street. 

2) The app never gets my current location when opening it. It always defaults to a location I was at a while ago, whether in report mode or the issues near me mode. You have to then hit the button to center it near you. That should be automatic.
title: Few bugs preventing a five star

review: I give this app for stars for two reasons one There is no iPad app two They change my city app and deleted it without putting a notice on the app saying to download this one I have to find out on my own imagine people that are not tech savvy and would even think about to do that. But this app is better than the website still think it could be improved.
title: See click fix St. Pete app gone

review: the burden on taxpayers to do the city's maintenance work, isn't the way city maintenance is supposed to happen. In return for tax dollars, the city should fix the problems such as illegal dumping and parking violations (which are obvious if the city took an hour to drive around the neighborhood) without making taxpayers take the time to report each issue piecemeal. Instead the city uses this app to pretend it's effectively listening to taxpayers when it doesn't even fix most of the problems reported. What a joke.
title: App doesn't let you submit requests

review: I want to thank See Click Fix and the staff in Lake Elsinore for their prompt and thorough work. Cleaning up other people junk is not a pleasant job, but our residents truly appreciate your efforts. You keep our community clean and presentable and ultimately improve our property value. Thank you for everything you do!
title: Thank you!

review: A big tree fell across the street and it took less than a half hour for them to show up with all the needed equipment to quickly and professionally clear the road. Friendly workers who cleaned up after themselves and had smiles the whole time. Thank you.
title: Incredibly great service. Really!

review:
Sometimes—like to report an abandoned car in your neighborhood—this app requires multiple tiny details, many of which I can only guess at

But usually it’s a quick way to alert the city to non-emergency stuff that needs attention: a new pothole dangerous to cyclists, street lights out, suspicious water flows (busted sewer?) etc
title: Mostly fine way to alert city officials

review:
I’m still getting the same notices over and over for issues I posted 2+ years ago that we’re fixed even though I indicate that they have been fixed and should be CLOSED.
This has worked great for all the issues I’ve posted and should get 5 stars for that except for the continual notices. 
I’ll continue to use it when something comes up but once it’s fixed and I show it’s closed PLEASE stop sending me the same messages.
title: Used to Be Great

review: This is the only way my county will apparently let me report a problem, but it doesn’t work.  I downloaded the app and followed the instructions only to be told I already had an account.  How is that possible when I’ve never used the app before?  I then tried to register using my husband’s e-mail address & waited for a one time password to be sent.  It never happened.  I guess this is now a convenient way for our local government folks to hide behind their desks and get paid for doing nothing!
title: Doesn’t Work!

review: Utility of the app is significantly reduced because the only way to attach a photo is most recent or camera. One cannot go to their library, and one cannot select multiple photos to upload. This functionality used to work in earlier versions of the app, but seems to not work. When trying to upload those photos from the iPhone library, the app goes back to the starting screen with no results. They need to fix this for the app to have any utility
title: Photos do not work right

review: I was able to photograph and locate an issue and submit the problem immediately to the city. On my morning walk the next day it had already been fixed. A wonderful useful and useable application!
title: Immediate notification of location and priblems

review: I used the app to take a photo of a water leak to report and it even showed the photo in the preview. As soon as I hit submit it choose the last photo from my camera album and not the one I just took in the app, which was a photo for work with sensitive data in view! Now I can’t even delete the post and it is available for anyone to view! Emailed the support group and flagged the post but still no take down. I’m furious and no way should this have been allowed to happen
title: Uploaded a wrong photo!

review:
Starts with 5 stars because it works!
Loses one star because it's hard to use and you have to click a bunch of extra steps to acknowledge things instead of making the form better. For example one could require a street corner or description from nearest GPS point to handle some poor GPS coordinates from phone. Thanks though!
title: Gets things done!

review:
While my hometown city doesn't use SeeClickFix for infrastructure deficiency reporting, I live in an area where 2 neighboring cities & 3rd city about an hour away uses SeeClickFix app platform. Instead of having multiple apps for multiple cities, government agencies, etc which use the SCF platform, this app does it all in one.
Update: Downgraded to 4 stars because the comments aren’t showing in the app.
title: Love this app

review:
When I moved to Oakland I was surprised at the condition of the roads. I mentioned seeing some garbage left on the sidewalk and a neighbor told me about SeeClickFix!  

For a test I wrote up about a pothole. Within a week it was filled!  What a surprise!  I’ve been reporting all sorts of incidents like debris, potholes, and sidewalk tripping hazards.  

I’m impressed with the promptness of the response to fix. I also learned that if anything may be hazardous waste to contact the fire department- who came by the very next day to pick up the 5-gallon cans of used paint! 

The app is easy to use, similar to the website reporting.
title: Grrrreat to know this app!

review:
The app is not perfect, but solid enough to get the job done…and it is so much better than previous systems I have used!

The app is only a tool that makes it much easier to report problems, so the very bad reviews based on how the cities actually respond (or don’t) to the reports are not at all fair to the creators of the app itself.
title: A good tool that cities may or may not use well

review: This used to be great but now I haven't been able to submit a report. The new "location validation" won't let me write the address and is making me try to choose an old report that was never closed that doesn't have the exact address. So when I hit submit I get an error message now saying the address isn't legitimate because it gives an address range and won't let me pinpoint a new location. (For illegal trash dumping). So many questions to ask and a waste of time now!
title: Used to work

review: I made an account and my city that I live in does use this and resolves issues quickly. However, I get several emails everyday of other posts in my neighborhood, have unsubscribed to everything, and messaged support THREE times now asking them to delete my account (it always tells me there is an error when I try to do it myself…). So, I would highly suggest just not using this platform unless you enjoy email spam and no support from their support email.
title: No support with app

review: Maybe it’s just that I have an old apple phone, but this Ap needs to remember the phone owner. It’s ridiculous have to type in your name, address and phone number each time. Also, the checks to acknowledge the rules is almost invisible and they are not on the same side of the page as other boxes. Make those boxes bold!
title: Needs to be more user friendly

review: I love the fact that there is a means for the public to report issues in Oakland that needs to be addressed. City employees can not be everywhere so having the public's eyes helps everyone who lives and works in Oakland. It has been my experience that the City responds in a timely manner whenever I've reported an issue.
title: Cleaner City

review: I have submitted three different street lights in my neighborhood that was not working. An within a few days all three were repair.  It's so much easier than having to call the city an get someone to listen.  Just click submit an your done.
title: Great app to fix street light

review:
It's easier to report on this app than on DC's 311 app, but on either one good luck getting better than a 10% success rate. I've reported around 150 items in the last 2 years in DC. Maybe 6 or 7 have been fixed. 

It's far too easy for cities to close out requests without doing anything at all. SeeClickFix should require a photo before cities can "close" a problem that has likely just been ignored.
title: Easy to report, hard to get anything fixed

review: The app makes reporting easy but one problem I noticed was if i see something that needs to be reported and I’m driving at the time, i can snap a picture but i need to pull over in order to submit because the app will always take the GPS address even if i type in the correct address of the violation it won’t accept it.
title: Good app

review: It’s all or none with this tool, at least it gives us a place to vent and at times it works great, the problem is we are all overwhelmed, correct me if I’m wrong, but I see we’re making ground on illegal dumping I don’t see it like I used to, and I don’t hear or see post that dumping is still a major problem, still happens, but don’t see it as much.
title: All Hands on Deck

review: Never had a problem. Easy to use. Provides a great service. I report potholes all the time. If you were no longer near the location, you can move the location of your report on the map. I would give it five stars, but I'm not 100% sure these reports or actually submitted to local governments or not.
title: Great, assuming reports are submitted

review: My SCF app fails consistently, most likely because they fail to keep up as OS's improve. I start a report and if there is a pic in it the app quits half way thru the report. The app has not been updated since 2014 according to this page.
title: Fails to keep current with OS

review: I placed a request to have 4 shopping carts removed that were on Promenade Ave. The very next day, I received info that the task had been completed and resolved. The issue was resolved very quickly.
title: Quick response and action taken

review: There needs to be a catch all  section for issues that don’t fit nicely into the selected issues. Dead fixed a water line break in front of my home last October/November and broke my curb and messed up my grass from the sidewalk to the street and I am unable to report this issue on the app. The work still has not been corrected and my front yard looks a mess. Please fix this issue
title: Need additional choices for issues to be reported

review: I sent a request last week to see, click, fix about the fire hazard danger of overgrown weeds/bushes on the fire road behind the houses on Sunmeadow St.  The crew literally had it cleared early the week after my request.  Thank you crew! Job well done!
title: Fire road hazard clearance -Sunmeadow St

review: After using this service for many years it seems more like a middle man to getting anything done, and many things seem to die with that middle man. If you have an issue in your town your better off heading down to the office to speak to a person the old fashioned way, or even giving a call would likely work better, heck sending a telegram might even work better! Overall I am not impressed by this App and find it has no need for existence.
title: Not Impressed

review: This is app is easy to use and allows you to put additional information to help explain the situation. Very helpful and creates a quick turn around time with the county while also providing feedback in a timely manner.
title: Great tool for the community

review:
No more calling since my daughter downloaded this app. 

Now I take a picture of the trash that others dump in my neighborhood and magically the fairy angels come and pick it up. Like magic, poof it’s gone, but there has been time that I had to resend. 

Thank you for this app City of Oakland.
title: Love this app

review: The app is extremely useful and effective when it works, but it has a lot of bugs with iOS 26. Entered text gets garbled when making selections from the drop downs. When it works, it works and if the reports are detailed, honest, and well intentioned, repairs often occur, which is great for the city and its residents. If they update the app for iOS 26, it will be much more useful, but they have not done that yet.
title: Good, when it works, but buggy in iOS 26

review: See click fix seems to be a useful tool for bringing non critical issues to the attention of city workers. In the hands of self-appointed watchdogs with beefs against their neighbors it is sometimes abused. Some of the issues seem like they would be better resolved by knocking on your neighbor’s door and discussing the matter. I wish it was possible to comment on individual complaints.
title: What’s the beef?

review: App works great, easy to send issues and follow up on them. Chicago is not integrated well into this, though. Many issues are not listed and  issues get closed by the City when they haven't been resolved.
title: Great app, which the city did more

review: This app works very well for my purposes to communicate property needs that should be reported .  Action is almost always taken and notifications are sent.
title: Easy effective system!

review: I find that with this app we get things done, especially if someone takes a picture. Thank you to all the City representatives that take the time to respond. #lovemycity #loveCorona
title: Love Click fix app

review: I am loving this app… Response time is quick, and the first few things I've reported were taken care of within a couple days or so. It really does work, and it is very easy to use. :-)
title: Great!

review: It is amazing the problems in the neighborhood that the CITY OF FOLSOM has been so proactive in taking care of certain situations in our neighborhoods. I. E. abandoned vehicles/ cars parked on the lawn / tall dried weeds that had the potential of a fire threatening homes.
title: Taking Care of Business

review:
Some agencies click “resolved” to indicate they have dispensed with the matter. It’s out of their hands, as if they fixed the issue. 

But sometimes they’ve only referred it. It is out of their hands; but to the person notifying via Click… it’s not resolved at all. 

Perhaps there could be more options for the agency. “Referred” — then STATE WHAT AGENCY so the customer can follow up if accountability is needed. Also, perhaps another something to justify any delay, like waiting for parts, waiting for the mission to return fr Mars, etc. 

Thank you. I really should give this five stars.
title: “Resolved” needs to be changed

review: This is a must for any county - it gives citizens the opportunity to report on things they see that need fixing or addressing. You may see a fallen sign or trash on the roadway. Now you can report it!
title: Great Tool!

review: A friend of mine recommended this app to report issues and it works so well. I submitted an issue yesterday and it was fixed today. What a great idea. Thank you guys for creating this app to make our cities and communities better!
title: I am so impressed!

review: I think it’s a really good site. They told us about this at the city that we saw something that was graffiti to report it. They were gonna clean up. We really appreciate this. Thank you.
title: Thank uou

review: I had my doubts about this when I first downloaded it and set up my account online.  The company is back East, I'm in California, how will they effect change across the country?  My first report was for a pothole during the rainy season and after posting it I noticed there were no city responses so I called the SeeClickFix company and the man I spoke with assured me they would get my city linked up to their system.  Sure enough, the response came in and the pothole was fixed within days -- and that was just the first report.  Oh, and I was the only person in my area doing this then.  A few weeks later I notice a street sign was broken - probably hit by a car - so I reported that and to my amazement, it was fixed in 3 days!  This is way more effective than making phone calls.  Potholes are fixed within 24 hours now and my reports are now acknowledged by up to 4 city departments.  I recently reported illegal commercial signage, specifically, a window washer company nailed signs on telephone posts in several places in the neighborhood and again, the response was immediate.  I love this app and highly recommend it - I've never had any problems with bugs in this app.

review: Used this app to complain about a bridge that was poorly lit in my neighborhood. From my phone I could see there were reports of muggings near one end of the bridge so I uploaded a quick report (my other friends seem to be able to upload fine from this app also) and was generally more aware and alert walking around there - bad news can be useful to know. Anyway, someone must have been paying attention as a bright light was soon put up overlooking that corner. Subsequently I did feel a lot safer walking that route home, more so than if had just used my lightsaber app :) Would be cool if I could use this to complain about issues in my apartment building.

review: This has been a great tool for reporting and tracking neighborhood issues--I'm always heartened to discover more people invested in the neighborhood's upkeep. Now if only Richmond could keep up with the reports--as more and more residents use it, the only drawback to the app is the dawning frustration as you wait longer and longer for an under-resourced city to address them. (Or, most aggravatingly, declare an unresolved issue closed!) but SeeClickFix itself is easy to use and helpful to the max.
title: Useful report record

review:
**UPDATE**

This app is ONLY AS GOOD AS THE CITY!  I have reported 20 issues from potholes, missing water main valve covers to broken water lines.  99% of the reports have been issued "Case Closed", "Case Completed" status and THE WORK WAS NEVER PREFORMED!!!  The City of Houston is reporting completed work when in fact it is NOT!!!  The City of Houston is not being held accountable! I don't believe  this is the fault of this app developer. Rather it's the problem of the broken city they call Houston.

---------

I just don't know affective this app actually is. I made a case then within minutes it was closed by the city when work was not preformed. Some of the complaints just show a picture and no description. I know this because one of my complaints is this way and I provided verbiage with it.  Just seems that the City of Houston just closes cases or keeps them open forever. Also I accidentally voted for my own cases... Does the city even see the votes? Update: sure enough the city closed yet another case that hasn't been resolved, the cities need to provide information on why the case is closed!  This app just makes you think your doing something when in reality we are not when the city is not being held accountable!!
title: Has potential But!

review:
I’ve used the app on a number of occasions and it misrepresents what the app does.

It says it notifies public officials BUT DOES NOT DO SO.

I had an issue open for six months and the representation is that relevant officials are notified. In Las Vegas, they are as follows:
WATCH AREAS NOTIFIED 
City of Las Vegas PLUS 
Clark County District A Commissioner Steve Sisolak 
An email was sent to this watcher. 
Clark County, Nevada 
An email was sent to this watcher. 
Downtown Fremont District PRO 
An email was NOT sent to this watcher. [Notify Anyway] 
jrvegas 
An email was NOT sent to this watcher. [Notify Anyway] 
Mayor of Las Vegas 
An email was sent to this watcher.

And after confusing messaging I receive the following pegged to one of my issues:

Sorry for the confusion. There has never been anyone watching those messages in the past. Those are report zones created by by someone with the app, but not an official entity. We are looking into changing that going forward though. I can't promise anything yet, but look for news on this soon.

If there is no one watching, why bother telling me there is? Did you pick names out of a hat to make it look like the mayor and my commissioner was being notified?

Waste of time… See, Click, Fix NOTHING.
title: More Suitable Name: SeeClick; no Fix

review: I found this app through a news page. The problem here in Cincinnati is that the cities aren't using the app. For example, the City of Cincinnati has its own app. So if you post an issue with this app, nothing will happen until the problem gets fixed by other means and the issue gets closed by another user. I don't have time to close others' issues or explain how to really get things fixed (street light out: go to Duke Energy's site for repair). If the developers linked this to other sites, like Duke's, it would be a lot more useful.
title: Nobody's responding to issues

review: My son has reported several potholes which has been fixed within days. Yesterday, I reported an overflowing garbage can that has been sitting across the street from a local high school for months untouched. I'm sure a private citizen placed it there with good intentions however once it was filled and overflowing it was forgotten! Today, it has been removed!!! Love this method to communicate with our county services!! Wish more communities utilized this technology!!

review: I love the idea of this app. I downloaded it hoping it would be easier to use than the website. It wasn't. The Map tab doesn't update when you pan around; you have to hit the refresh button constantly. Also, there appears to be no way to update existing issues. Many issues have no photos associated with them, and many others have been resolved but are still listed as open. I'd like to help out, but I'm not given a way to do so.

Thanks!!

review:
AWESOME!!!

The system for placing your issue on the map is INGENIOUS! I was expecting to have to try to tap a precise location on this tiny little screen; instead, all you have to do is drag the map while the marker remains stationary in the center. Whoever came up with that has my gratitude and admiration.

Overall, the app is very intuitive and useful.
title: Wow

review:
I so want to like this app more, but it feels empty and lacking leadership.

FourSquare has Mayors, Yelp has Elite, but what this app needs is a way to hold our local leaders accountable.  Each issue reported should have a field that uses a wiki database to let the app notify the person in charge.  In areas with no leadership you should sponsor volunteer crews. In most big cities outside agencies are contracted for shopping carts and litter. Let your database be updated with this info.  Have a blog where you showcase cities where this is working.

review: I was forced to use the app to report an issue to my city because SeeClickFix makes website reporting non-functional. I prefer to use web interfaces - mobile or laptop/desktop - to limit single-use app clutter on my phone, and to limit the data collection that apps generally are designed to maximize. SeeClickFix.com does have a web interface to report issues but the issue reporting function is hidden and not obvious. Instead the site devotes its real estate and resources to directing users to download the app. Once the website reporting page is found, it fails to function. I tried it on mobile Firefox, desktop Firefox and desktop Safari. Only after failing those three attempts did I download the app, because there is no alternative way to report a road construction problem in my city. I reported the issue and then deleted the app.  There is no need for this app - issues should be reportable through the website.  Some users may find it convenient but I see no reason why the issue reporting page could not be bookmarked in the browser of the user’s choice.
title: Unnecessary app

I like tag idea of this app a lot, but sadly, there are problems. 

I had to delete it and redownload it because it failed to launch correctly. 

Submissions are "moderated"! Why?  I do not need an editor to change my submission. You need to explain why you do this. 

While the app works OK, the City Of Oakland does not, and the app's input falls mostly in deaf ears. The app company should add code to continuously follow up with Oakland to get results. As such, this is not much better than emailing the city directly. 

Lastly, I want to be able to post multiple images. 

Only a few stars. Good idea? OK technology, poor execution.
